elections.note: Certified final 926,363 ballots cast (CA SoS Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County: Precinct 416,188 + Vote-by-Mail 510,175 = 926,363). Election-night first-report count not sourceable: San Diego ROV's 2010 live results were served from the sdcounty.ca.gov/sdvote.com county site and were NOT archived on election night (Wayback has no Nov 2-3, 2010 results-page capture; only post-election semi-official snapshots survive). The DFM/livevoterturnout ENR system whose pages ARE archivable did not begin in San Diego until 2018. Pre-epollbook (San Diego deployed e-pollbooks in 2022); pre-ASV (2024).

Certified final 1,203,265 ballots cast (CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County: Precinct 528,258 + Vote-by-Mail 675,007 = 1,203,265). Election-night first-report count not sourceable: San Diego ROV's 2012 live results page was not archived on election night (only post-election semi-official captures exist on the county site). The livevoterturnout ENR system began in 2018. Pre-epollbook, pre-ASV.

Certified final 692,434 ballots cast (CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County: Precinct 242,638 + Vote-by-Mail 449,796 = 692,434). Election-night first-report count not sourceable: San Diego ROV's 2014 live results page was not archived on election night. The livevoterturnout ENR system began in 2018. Pre-epollbook, pre-ASV.

Certified final 1,346,513 ballots cast (CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County: Precinct 489,576 + Vote-by-Mail 856,937 = 1,346,513). Election-night first-report count not sourceable: San Diego ROV's 2016 live results were the XSL-transformed page sdvote.com/content/rov/electioninfo/transform.htm?paramVal1=election.xsl, whose earliest Wayback capture is 2016-11-19 (semi-official, 11 days post-election), with NO election-night (Nov 8-9) snapshot. The livevoterturnout ENR system began in 2018. Pre-epollbook, pre-ASV.

Election-night value 536,734 ballots from the FIRST archived capture of San Diego ROV's live results page (livevoterturnout.com/SanDiego/LiveResults Index_5), Wayback snapshot 2018-11-07 12:06 UTC = ~4:06 a.m. PT, rendered via render_wayback.cjs. CAVEAT: that capture shows 'Ballots Cast 536,734' with 1,446 of 2,136 precincts (68%) reporting — it is a mid-/late-night cumulative that already includes most election-day precinct ballots, NOT the clean 8 p.m. first tranche (no 8 p.m. snapshot was archived). So 536,734/1,173,924 = 45.72% is an UPPER BOUND that overstates the true post-poll-close first report and is not directly comparable to the 2022/2024 first-tranche figures. Certified final 1,173,924 (CA SoS: Precinct 369,655 + Vote-by-Mail 804,269). Pre-epollbook, pre-ASV.

Election-night first report = 487,957 ballots (Mail 468,632 + Vote Center 19,325) from San Diego ROV's official ENR page (livevoterturnout.com/ENR/sandiegocaenr Index_16, 'Statewide General Election'), Wayback snapshot 2022-11-09 05:50 UTC = ~9:50 p.m. PT, the FIRST archived capture after the 8 p.m. poll close, rendered via render_wayback.cjs. The low Vote Center count (19,325 = early-voting only) confirms this is the pre-Election-Day mail + early-vote-center first tranche, not a later election-day update. 487,957/1,043,490 = 46.76%. Post-epollbook: San Diego deployed electronic pollbooks at vote centers, running under the Voter's Choice Act all-mail/vote-center model (a confound that independently front-loads ballots into the pre-Election-Day mail batch). Pre-ASV. Certified final 1,043,490 (CA SoS: Precinct 104,388 + Vote-by-Mail 939,102).

Election-night first report = 855,948 ballots (Mail 787,702 + Vote Center 68,246) from San Diego ROV's official ENR page (livevoterturnout.com/ENR/sandiegocaenr Index_20), Wayback snapshot 2024-11-06 04:35 UTC = ~8:35 p.m. PT, the first post-close capture (clean first tranche: pre-Election-Day mail + early vote-center ballots), rendered via render_wayback.cjs. 855,948/1,503,018 = 56.95%. Post-epollbook AND post-ASV: San Diego adopted automated signature verification for the 2024 cycle (e-pollbooks + Voter's Choice Act vote centers since 2022). Certified final 1,503,018 (CA SoS: Precinct 227,714 + Vote-by-Mail 1,275,304).

notes: Non-COVID PRE-2020 baseline (2010, 2012, 2014, 2016, 2018) plus 2022 and 2024; the 2020 general is deliberately EXCLUDED as an early-voting/COVID outlier. Definition: election-night % = ballots in San Diego ROV's FIRST post-poll-close report (mail received before Election Day + early vote-center ballots, ~8 p.m. PT) divided by CA SoS certified final ballots cast (Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County, 'Total Ballots Cast' = Precinct + Vote-by-Mail). DENOMINATORS are PRIMARY and solid for all seven generals (SoS participation tables). NUMERATORS: clean PRIMARY first-tranche counts exist only for 2022 (487,957 @ ~9:50 p.m.) and 2024 (855,948 @ ~8:35 p.m.), recovered from San Diego's live ENR pages on livevoterturnout.com/ENR/sandiegocaenr via the Wayback Machine + render_wayback.cjs puppeteer helper (WebFetch is blocked for web.archive.org but curl/CDX and headless Chrome are not). 2018 has only a ~4 a.m. cumulative (536,734, 68% precincts reporting) from the earlier livevoterturnout.com/SanDiego/LiveResults system (SECONDARY, overstates the 8 p.m. first tranche). 2010-2016 have NO sourceable election-night first-report count: San Diego's pre-2018 live results pages (sdcounty.ca.gov/sdvote.com county site, and in 2016 the AEM XSL page sdvote.com/content/rov/electioninfo/transform.htm?paramVal1=election.xsl) were not captured by Wayback on election night (only post-election semi-official snapshots survive), and the archivable DFM/livevoterturnout ENR system did not begin in San Diego until 2018 — so those four are null/confidence-none with solid certified denominators. ADOPTION: e-pollbooks 2022, automated signature verification (ASV) 2024; so 2010-2018 are PRE-epollbook, 2022/2024 are POST-epollbook, and only 2024 is POST-ASV. CONFOUND: San Diego's move to electronic pollbooks (2022) coincided with the Voter's Choice Act all-mail/vote-center rollout, which independently shifts ballots into the pre-Election-Day mail batch that constitutes the first report, so the higher post-2022 first-report shares cannot be attributed to e-pollbooks alone. Like-to-like first-report shares where sourceable: PRESIDENTIAL 2024 (post, post-ASV) = 56.95% vs no sourceable pre-2020 presidential numerator (2012/2016 null); MIDTERM 2022 (post) = 46.76% vs 2018 (pre) ~45.72% UPPER BOUND (4 a.m./68%-precinct cumulative, not a clean 8 p.m. comparison).
